questions.question: Write a client-server program in C programming language that allows the client to send the file name and receives the contents of the requested file from the server. The server checks if the file exists and sends its contents back to the client if present. The client should handle the case where the file is not found. Provide a detailed explanation of your code with explanations and comments.
questions.hint: Focus on error checking and handling, file existence checks, and proper client-server communication.

questions.question: Compare the advantages and disadvantages of using sockets for client-server communication in C programming language compared to using threads for inter-process communication. Provide examples to support your arguments.
questions.hint: Focus on performance, reliability, and complexity of implementation.

questions.question: Design a client-server program in C programming language that allows a user to download a file from a server using the client's file name. The server maintains a list of available files and their corresponding paths. Provide a detailed explanation of your design with explanations and comments.
questions.hint: Focus on data structures, file handling, and client-server communication protocols.
